data.title: JAGX: Net loss widened to $30.8M on flat revenue and higher expenses, with liquidity concerns persisting
data.description:
Net revenue for the nine months ended September 30, 2025 was $8.3M, with a net loss of $30.8M as operating expenses rose 6.8% year-over-year. Cash at period end was $3.5M, and management warns of substantial doubt about the ability to continue as a going concern without additional financing.

Original document: Jaguar Health, Inc. [JAGX] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 17 2025

Disclaimer

data.title: OMQS: Net loss narrowed on lower revenue after a major asset sale, but liquidity risks persist
data.description:
Revenue declined 10% year-over-year to $24.2 million, but net loss narrowed to $784,000 due to cost controls and a major asset sale. Liquidity remains a concern, with a working capital deficit of $11.8 million and ongoing debt covenant noncompliance.

Original document: OMNIQ Corp. [OMQS] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 21 2025

Disclaimer

data.title: TOMI Environmental's Q3 revenue rises sequentially
data.description:
Overview

  • TOMI Q3 revenue of $2 mln missed analyst expectations

  • Company reported a 95% sequential revenue increase in Q3 2025

  • Gross profit margin remained strong at 61% in Q3

Outlook

  • Company expects to close $3 mln in pending contracts before year-end

  • TOMI sees potential demand of $15 mln for product offerings

  • Company positioned to capitalize on global trends in clean manufacturing and biosecurity

Result Drivers

  • INTERNATIONAL SALES - International sales accounted for 36% of Q3 revenue, up from 20% in Q2

  • RECURRING SALES - Strong recurring BIT™ Solution sales drove revenue growth

  • PRODUCT INNOVATION - Ongoing R&D investment to expand SteraMist applications

Key Details

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q3 Sales

Miss

$2 mln

$2.74 mln (1 Analyst)

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "buy"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the environmental services & equipment peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for TOMI Environmental Solutions Inc is $3.50, about 76.9% above its November 13 closing price of $0.81

data.title: Stonegate Capital Partners Updates Coverage on Aquafil Group (ECNL) Q3 2025
data.description:
Dallas, Texas--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) - Aquafil Group (MI: ECNL): Stonegate Capital Partners updates coverage on Aquafil Group (MI: ECNL). In 3Q25, ECNL delivered resilient profitability despite softer top-line trends, highlighting the benefits of ongoing efficiency measures and cost control. The Company achieved an EBITDA margin of 13.7%, up from 12.1% in 3Q24, primarily reflecting lower raw material costs, disciplined cost-containment actions, and a richer mix of regenerated products. ECONYL®- branded and other regenerated fibers continued to play a pivotal role, with these products representing roughly 60% of fiber revenues year to date. Regionally, the North American BCF business remained a key growth engine with solid volume gains, EMEA was broadly in line with the prior year, and Asia Pacific remained soft, particularly in textile applications. Management also advanced its reorganization of U.S. carpet collection and recycling operations, recognizing one-off restructuring charges which position the business for structurally lower labor and logistics costs in FY26 and beyond.

To view the full announcement, including downloadable images, bios, and more, click here.

Key Takeaways:

  • ECONYL products accounted for 60.2% of fiber revenues in 3Q25
  • Disciplined cost controls and the ongoing reorganization of U.S. carpet collection and recycling operations expected to continue benefiting margins into FY26
  • Strong EBITDA margin of 13.7%, up from 12.1% in 3Q24

Click image above to view full announcement.

About Stonegate

Stonegate Capital Partners is a leading capital markets advisory firm providing investor relations, equity research, and institutional investor outreach services for public companies. Our affiliate, Stonegate Capital Markets (member FINRA) provides a full spectrum of investment banking, equity research and capital raising for public and private companies.

data.title: Fitch Revises FEMSA's Outlook to Stable; Affirms IDRs at 'A'
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Monterrey-14 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Fomento Economico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V.'s (FEMSA) Long-Term Foreign and Local Currency Issuer Default Ratings (IDR) and senior unsecured debt at 'A'. The Rating Outlook on the IDR was revised to Stable from Negative. Fitch has also affirmed FEMSA's National Scale long-term rating and local issuances at 'AAA(mex)', as well as its National Scale short-term rating at 'F1+(mex)'. The Rating Outlook on the National Scale is Stable.

The Outlook revision reflects a deleverage trend in FEMSA's EBITDAR leverage and our expectation that it will be close or below 2.5x in the next 18 to 24 months.

FEMSA's ratings reflect its solid retail operations in Latin America, through its Proximity Americas, Health, and Fuel divisions, and in Europe, through Proximity Europe. The ratings incorporate its equity ownership in Coca-Cola FEMSA, S.A.B. de C.V. (KOF), which provides a stable inflow of dividends at the holding level.

Key Rating Drivers

Leverage Gradually Improving: Fitch projects FEMSA's EBITDAR leverage, defined as total debt adjusted by leases for FEMSA excluding KOF to EBITDAR plus dividends from KOF, to gradually decline below 2.5x by 2027. The projection incorporates sustained EBITDAR growth from its retail operations, stable dividend inflows from KOF, and modest debt reduction. Fitch also expects that FEMSA's EBITDAR net leverage will remain at low levels at around 1.5x, after shareholder's distributions of close to USD3.8 billion in 2025 and 2026.

Manageable Operating Headwinds: FEMSA's retail operating results in 2025 are within Fitch's previous projections despite a more challenging consumer and competitive environment in Mexico, which has impacted store traffic. Fitch expects the company to maintain strategies oriented toward affordability and cost savings to overcome these headwinds. We forecast for 2025-2027 revenue growth of approximately 8%, with EBITDAR margins of around 11%.

Solid Retail Position in Latin America: FEMSA holds a strong position in Mexico's retail sector through its OXXO stores, with a growing presence in South America, including Brazil, Colombia, Chile, Ecuador and Peru. As of Sept. 30, 2025, OXXO had 24,057 stores in Mexico, making it the country's second-largest retailer by sales, as well as 1,079 stores in South America and 242 in U.S. Additionally, FEMSA's Health division had 4,391 drugstores in Mexico, Chile, Colombia, and Ecuador, while its Fuel business operated 558 gas stations in Mexico.

Retail Business in Europe: Fitch believes FEMSA's retail operations in Europe improve its geographic diversification. Its Proximity Europe division is an important participant in the convenience store and food service markets in Europe, with operations in Switzerland, Germany, Luxembourg, the Netherlands and Austria. The company has approximately 2,777 multi-format stores that are mostly operated as franchises under a recognized brand portfolio. This division also provides financial services on a digital platform and has 16 pretzel production lines.

No Mexico Country Ceiling Cap: FEMSA's ratings benefit from sufficient cash flow generation from overseas to cover hard currency (HC) interest expenses over the rating horizon. Its 'A' Foreign Currency IDR will be supported by the applicable Country Ceiling of Switzerland, at 'AAA', given Proximity's Europe EBITDA, which is sufficient to cover HC interest expenses. Additionally, its external debt service coverage is supported by readily available cash overseas and EBITDA from drugstores in Chile and Colombia. However, downgrades of Mexico's sovereign ratings and Country Ceiling could pressure FEMSA's ratings.

Peer Analysis

FEMSA's credit profile is among the strongest among Latin American corporates. This results from its solid retail operations in Latin America and Europe and its equity ownership in KOF, which generate solid cash flow and dividends that allow FEMSA to maintain its healthy financial position at the holding company level.

FEMSA's ratings are comparable with other Mexican peers in the 'A' rating category, such as Arca Continental, S.A.B. de C.V. (A/Stable) and America Movil, S.A.B. de C.V. (A-/Stable). These companies have solid market positions in their industries, geographically diversified operations, and strong financial positions with low leverage and strong liquidity. FEMSA's EBITDAR net leverage of around 1.5x in the long term is projected to be similar than that of Arca Continental or America Movil, which both have leverage at or below 1.5x.

Compared with other global companies in the retail sector, such as Walmart, Inc. (AA/Stable) and Target Corporation (A/Negative), FEMSA has lower scale, more exposure to emerging markets and a EBITDAR leverage at around 2.5x. Walmart is expected to maintain EBITDAR leverage close to 2.0x, while Target at 2.5x. FEMSA is rated higher than peers such as Tesco PLC (BBB/Stable) and Cencosud S.A. (BBB/Stable), as it has maintained higher profitability margins, lower leverage and stronger financial flexibility across the rating horizon. No Country Ceiling constraint and no operating environment influence were applied to FEMSA's ratings.

Key Assumptions

--Revenue increase (FEMSA excluding KOF) averaging 8% in 2025-2027;

--EBITDAR margin (FEMSA excluding KOF) averaging close to 11% in 2025-2027;

--Annual Capex (FEMSA excluding KOF) averaging MXN24 billion in 2025-2027;

--Annual dividends received from KOF averaging MXN8 billion in 2025-2027;

--Ordinary dividends payments to shareholders averaging MXN15 billion in 2025-2027;

--Extraordinary dividends and shares repurchase of MXN46.2 billion in 2025, and additional shareholder distributions of around MXN25 billion in 2026;

-- Proceeds from divestitures of around MXN14 billion in 2025.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade:

--Sustained deterioration of operating performance and profitability;

--A significant decline in the flow of dividends received from KOF;

--Debt-financed acquisitions; or return of considerable excess cash to shareholders above Fitch's projections;

--Sustained EBITDAR leverage (FEMSA Excluding KOF) above 2.5x;

--Sustained EBITDAR net leverage (FEMSA Excluding KOF) above 2.0x;

--A downgrade of Mexico's sovereign ratings and country ceiling.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade:

Fitch does not foresee any positive rating action over the medium term, but factors that are positive to FEMSA's credit profile include:

--Improved geographical and product diversification;

--Lower operating environment risks;

-- Maintenance of low leverage, robust cash flow and liquidity.

Liquidity and Debt Structure

FEMSA's cash balance and short-term investments, excluding KOF, as of Sept. 30, 2025, were MXN114 billion, with only MXN13.7 billion of short-term debt. The next significant debt amortizations related to senior notes are MXN11.5 billion in 2026 and 2027; and MXN6.2 billion in 2028. Fitch believes FEMSA has sufficient financial flexibility and access to bank loans and capital markets to face its debt requirements.

Issuer Profile

FEMSA is one of the largest companies in Mexico and Latin America with a diversified portfolio of companies in the beverage (Coca-Cola FEMSA), retail (Proximity Americas, Proximity Europe, Health and Fuel) and digital industries.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. MACROECONOMIC ASSUMPTIONS AND SECTOR FORECASTS Click here to access Fitch's latest quarterly Global Corporates Sector Forecasts Monitor data file which aggregates key data points used in our credit analysis. Fitch's macroeconomic forecasts, commodity price assumptions, default rate forecasts, sector key performance indicators and sector-level forecasts are among the data items included. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: S&P Global upgrades South Africa's long-term foreign currency rating to 'BB'
data.description:
S&P Global on Friday upgraded South Africa's foreign currency long-term sovereign credit rating to "BB" from "BB-", citing stronger growth prospects, an improving fiscal outlook and lower contingent liabilities following performance improvements at the state-owned electricity utility, Eskom.

South Africa is now two notches below investment grade on the foreign currency rating.

data.title: Tsodilo Resources Intends to Pursue Re-Domiciliation to United States
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) - Tsodilo Resources Limited  (OTCQB: TSDRF) (FSE: TZO) ("Tsodilo" or the "Company") announces that it intends to pursue re-domiciliation to the United States.

Tsodilo's operations are based in the Republic of Botswana, and a vast plurality of its shareholders are non-Canada based.

About Tsodilo Resources Limited

Tsodilo Resources Limited is an international resource exploration company engaged in the search for economic metal deposits at its Gcwihaba Resources (Pty) Limited ("Gcwihaba") projects in Botswana. The Company has a 100% stake in its Gcwihaba project area consisting of five metal (base, precious, platinum group, and rare earth) prospecting licenses all located in the North-West district of Botswana.

This press release may contain forward-looking statements. All statements, other than statements of historical fact, that address activities, events or developments that the Company believes, expects or anticipates will or may occur in the future (including, without limitation, statements pertaining to the use of proceeds, the impact of strategic partnerships and statements that describe the Company's future plans, objectives or goals) are forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements reflect the current expectations or beliefs of the Company based on information currently available to the Company. Forward- looking statements are subject to a number of risks and uncertainties that may cause the actual results of the Company to differ materially from those discussed in the forward-looking statements, and even if such actual results are realized or substantially realized, there can be no assurance that they will have the expected consequences to, or effects on the Company. Factors that could cause actual results or events to differ materially from current expectations include, among other things, changes in equity markets, changes in general economic conditions, market volatility, political developments in Botswana and surrounding countries, changes to regulations affecting the Company's activities, uncertainties relating to the availability and costs of financing needed in the future, exploration and development risks, the uncertainties involved in interpreting exploration results and the other risks involved in the mineral exploration business. Any forward-looking statement speaks only as of the date on which it is made and, except as may be required by applicable securities laws, the Company disclaims any intent or obligation to update any forward-looking statement, whether as a result of new information, future events or results or otherwise. data.title: Schedule for US economic data delayed by government shutdown
data.description:
The statistical agencies responsible for publishing data about the U.S. economy have begun updating release schedules following the reporting lapse that occurred during the federal government shutdown.

Following are the latest release dates for data that was missed during the shutdown and any schedule changes for future data related to that.

data.title: Modiv Industrial: Q3 2025 net income was $1.1M, FFO $4.2M, and AFFO $4.5M, with 47.7% leverage and 96% occupancy
data.description:
Portfolio is 82% industrial, 96% occupied, with $38.9M ABR and 14.2-year WALT. Q3 2025 net income was $1.1M, FFO $4.2M, and AFFO $4.5M, with a $4.0M impairment charge and $6.5M in preferred stock repurchases. Leverage is 47.7%, and all debt is fixed for 2025.

Original document: Modiv Industrial SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 14 2025

data.title: Modiv Industrial Q3 revenue slightly misses estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • Modiv Q3 revenue slightly missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted FFO for Q3 beat consensus estimates

Outlook

  • Company plans to recycle select assets to improve AFFO and strengthen balance sheet

  • Modiv sees potential inflection points in REIT market due to investor fatigue

Result Drivers

  • COST REDUCTIONS - Decrease in G&A, property expenses, and interest expenses contributed to 22% increase in AFFO

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

Q3 Revenue

Slight Miss*

$11.7 mln

$11.8 mln (3 Analysts)

Q3 Adjusted FFO

Beat

$4.5 mln

$0.34 (4 Analysts)

*Applies to a deviation of less than 1%; not applicable for per-share numbers.

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 6 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the commercial reits peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Modiv Industrial Inc is $17.75, about 17.7% above its November 13 closing price of $14.60

  • The stock recently traded at 140 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 124 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: RNAZ: Net loss increased to $21.2 million, but new capital and an acquisition extended cash runway into Q4 2026
data.description:
Net loss widened to $21.2 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, driven by higher R&D and clinical trial costs. A $25 million investment and the acquisition of a Phase 3-ready melanoma vaccine candidate extended the cash runway into Q4 2026.

Original document: TransCode Therapeutics, Inc. [RNAZ] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Nov. 20 2025

data.description:

  • Inhibikase Q3 net loss widens to $11.9 mln, driven by increased R&D and SG&A expenses

  • Cash reserves drop to $77.3 mln from $97.5 mln at year-end 2024

Result Drivers

  • EXECUTIVE TRANSITIONS - SG&A expenses rose due to executive transitions, including severance costs
